title: 12% SDS-PAGE Western Blot Protocol
author: Grace Basa
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rwrd7d6
materials: Western Blot Gel Pouring Apparatus (stands, glass plates, holder, combs)
Western blot running container (lid, electrodes, gel holders, buffer dam)
Western blot transfer apparatus (tank, lid, stir bar, ice pack, cassette, sponges)

steps.title: Clean Glass Plates
steps.instruction: Spray the glass plates with 70% ethanol before use and dry.

steps.title: Gather Gel Pouring Equipment
steps.instruction: Obtain materials Western Blot Gel Pouring Apparatus: green casting stands, 4 large glass plates, 4 small glass plates, plastic Western Blot holder for the casting stands, and combs.

steps.title: Assemble Gel Casting Stands
steps.instruction: Place small plate on top of the large plates. Slide these two glasses into the green casting stands, making sure that the glass plates are even. Close the casting stand and clip them in place on the plastic Western Blot gel holders.

steps.title: Prepare Gel Reagents
steps.instruction: Grab the following materials for 12% SDS-PAGE gels: Lower buffer (Tris 0.5M-pH 8.8), upper buffer (Tris 1.5M-pH 6.8), water, 30% Acrylamide-Bis 37.5:1, 10% SDS, 10% AP, and TEMED.

steps.title: Prepare Resolving Gel Solution
steps.instruction: Make the resolving gel first. Follow the recipe below. I usually make 4 gels at a time. SDS-PAGE gel recipes %s %s *Lower buffer: 1.5M Tris base pH8.8 Weigh 90.85g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH8.8 with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O. **Upper buffer: 0.5M Tris pH6.8 Weigh 30.25g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH6.8 with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O.

substeps.instruction: Make the resolving gel first. Follow the recipe below. I usually make 4 gels at a time. SDS-PAGE gel recipes %s %s *Lower buffer: 1.5M Tris base pH
Weigh 90.85g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH
with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O. **Upper buffer: 0.5M Tris pH
Weigh 30.25g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH
with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O.

steps.title: Pour Resolving Gel and Polymerize
steps.instruction: After making the resolving gel, use a 1000 microliter pipette to distribute the solution from the tube into the space between the glass plates. Stop pipetting the solution when you reach the bottom of the green section of the casting stand behind the glass plates. After pouring the gels, pipette some isopropanol on the top of the resolving gel to prevent evaporation. Let it polymerize for about 30 minutes or so. Use leftover solution from tube as a check to whether the gel is done polymerizing.

steps.title: Prepare Stacking Gel Solution
steps.instruction: While the resolving gel is polymerizing, make the stacking gel but don't add the TEMED until you're ready to pour the stacking gel in. Follow the recipe below for the stacking gel. SDS-PAGE gel recipes %s %s *Lower buffer: 1.5M Tris base pH8.8 Weigh 90.85g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH8.8 with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O. **Upper buffer: 0.5M Tris pH6.8 Weigh 30.25g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH6.8 with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O.

substeps.instruction: While the resolving gel is polymerizing, make the stacking gel but don't add the TEMED until you're ready to pour the stacking gel in. Follow the recipe below for the stacking gel. SDS-PAGE gel recipes %s %s *Lower buffer: 1.5M Tris base pH
Weigh 90.85g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH
with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O. **Upper buffer: 0.5M Tris pH
Weigh 30.25g of Tris base, dissolve in 350ml H 2 O. Adjust to pH
with concentrated HCl. Make up to 500ml with H 2 O.

steps.title: Remove Isopropanol
steps.instruction: Once the resolving gel is done polymerizing, dump out the isopropanol in the sink and rinse with water.

steps.title: Pour Stacking Gel and Polymerize
steps.instruction: Add the TEMED to your stacking gel solution and distribute the solution with the pipette until you reach the top of the short glass plate. Add the comb of your choice and make sure there is no bubbles. Let it polymerize for about 30 minutes. Use your tube of left over solution as a check.

steps.title: Store Polymerized Gels
steps.instruction: After the gel is done polymerizing, you can store gels by wrapping them with moist paper towel and placing them in 4 degrees Celsius fridge until you're ready to use them.

steps.title: Gather Gel Running Materials
steps.instruction: Grab materials for running a gel: Western blot running container, lid with electrodes, 1X tank buffer, gels, samples, buffer dam (if running one gel), and gel holders.

steps.title: Assemble Running Gel in Tank
steps.instruction: Place gels inside the gel holders and have the short plate and wells facing the interior of the gel holder.If you are only running one gel, use a buffer dam on the other side of your gels. Close them in place. Place the gels inside the tank.

steps.title: Denature Samples
steps.instruction: Before running the gel, make sure your samples have been denatured with sample buffer. If not, add the sample buffer to your samples and boil them for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Fill Tank with Running Buffer
steps.instruction: Fill the tank with 1X tank buffer until it reaches the appropriate line for how many gels you are running. Fill the interior of the gel holder with tank buffer to the very top.

steps.title: Load Ladder and Samples
steps.instruction: Load 10 microliters of the ladder of your choice into the first well. Transfer all your samples from the Eppendorf tubes into the wells of your gel.

steps.title: Connect Power Supply
steps.instruction: Place the lid on top of your Western blot apparatus with the electrodes matching with the corresponding color. Plug cables into the corresponding colors on the Western blot machines.

steps.title: Run SDS-PAGE Gel
steps.instruction: Run the gel for 1 hour at 160V. Check on your gel every 8-10 minutes to make sure your gel isn't leaking with the tank buffer. Pause the machine and use a pipette to move buffer from the tank into the gel holder if it's leaking.

steps.title: Gather Transfer Materials
steps.instruction: Grab the following materials to make the transfer 'sandwich': transfer tank and lid, small magnetic stir bar, ice pack, transfer electrodegel cassette, 2 sponges, Whatman blot paper, Immobilon membrane, 1X transfer buffer, methanol, one plastic tub to fit membrane, and another tub to fill with transfer buffer.

steps.title: Remove Gel from Plates
steps.instruction: Dislodge your gel from the glass plates after the run. Use a razor blase to cut away the stacking gel.

steps.title: Activate Immobilon Membrane
steps.instruction: Fill a small plastic tub with methanol and pre-wet the Immobilon membrane with it to activate it.
